```json
{
  "mnemonics": ["W G.I.R.L. P.I.C. (Waste Generation, Handling, ILRS, Processing, Recovery, Disposal)"],
  "keyPoints": ["Waste Generation: The activities that create solid waste.", "Collection: The gathering and transport of waste from generation sources.", "Transfer and Transport: Moving waste from collection points to processing or disposal sites.", "Processing and Recovery: Treating waste to recover materials or energy (e.g., recycling, composting).", "Disposal: The final placement of waste, typically in a landfill or waste-to-energy facility."],
  "commonMistakes": ["Thinking 'recovery' only means recycling. It also includes composting and energy recovery (Waste-to-Energy).", ["Forgetting that 'handling, separation, and storage' at the source (e.g., in your home) is a distinct and crucial first functional element.", "Assuming that 'collection' also includes processing. They are two separate functional elements."]
}
```
